# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 147

This is turn 147. Turns 1 through 146 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 147.

## Turn 147 — Proposition III passes the functorial Wall–Kummer audit

The frozen \(h_0\) submission remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation remains implicit. In the proof, \(40491355905\) denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); it is serialized by that integer only for the prescribed verifier modulus. 

Turn 146 independently closed Proposition II and left Proposition III—the natural determinant-pushout and Wall–Kummer sign theorem—as the remaining source audit. 

### 1. Exact \(h_0\) evaluation gives the asserted quadratic functor

Let \(V\) be a nontrivial simple self-dual tame module, \(q:V\to\mathbf F_2\) an invariant nonsingular quadratic form, and \(b\) its polar form. Let \(V_q\) be the associated extraspecial central extension.

Write \(X,D\in V_q\) for the coefficient components of \(x_0,d_0\), and let \(\varphi\) be conjugation by \(g_0\). Direct collection in an arbitrary class-two, exponent-four coefficient group gives the exact identity

\[
h_0=\varphi(X)\,X\,D^{-1}\varphi(D).
\tag{147.1}
\]

This is an equality in the full extraspecial group, not merely an equality of Hessians.

There are two projector branches.

- If \(P=0\), then \(D\) and \(X\) have the same image modulo the center, so \(D=ZX\) for a central involution \(Z\). Equation (147.1) gives \(h_0=\varphi(X)^2=X^2\).
- If \(P=1\), then \(T=1\), and the \(2\)-primary Frobenius \(U=S^{\omega _2}\) acts trivially on the simple characteristic-two module. Conjugation by \(\sigma_2\) is therefore a central automorphism of \(V_q\), whose square is trivial; since \(g_0=\sigma_2^2\), \(\varphi=1\), and again \(h_0=X^2\).

Thus the exact \(h_0\)-contribution is \(q(c)\). The commutator \(c_0=[d_0,z_0]\) contributes nothing in the \(P=1\) branch and contributes \(b(c,U^{-1}c)\) in the \(P=0\) branch. Consequently

\[
Q_{h,q}(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+b(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\qquad U=S^{\omega _2}.
\tag{147.2}
\]

This recovers the determinant formula from the exact submitted word. 

Its ramified polarization is

\[
b_U(c,c')
 =b\!\left(c,(1+U+U^{-1})c'\right).
\]

The operator \(1+U+U^{-1}=U^{-1}(U^2+U+1)\) is invertible: \(U\) has \(2\)-power order, while \(X^2+X+1\) is coprime to every power of \(X+1\). Hence \(Q_{h,q}\) is nonsingular.

As a fresh finite sanity check, I exhaustively tested the Wall identity below for every \(2\)-power-order orthogonal transformation on both Arf classes in dimensions \(2\) and \(4\); no exception occurred.

### 2. Every rank-one zero-edge pushout really descends to a nonsingular simple-head form

At the terminal nonlinear recurrence node, retain

\[
R=\Phi(K)\simeq C_2,\qquad
M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,\qquad
V=M/T_0,
\]

where \(V\) is the unique nontrivial simple head.

The unique nonzero outer Fourier character \(\lambda:R\to\mathbf F_2\) produces a nonsplit central extension

\[
1\longrightarrow \mathbf F_2
\longrightarrow K_\lambda
\longrightarrow M
\longrightarrow 1
\]

with square map \(q_\lambda:M\to\mathbf F_2\) and polar form \(b_\lambda\).

First,

\[
b_\lambda(T_0,M)=0.
\tag{147.3}
\]

Indeed, \(b_\lambda^\flat:T_0\to M^\vee\) is a module map. Every composition factor of \(T_0\) is trivial, whereas

\[
\operatorname{soc}(M^\vee)
 \simeq \operatorname{head}(M)^\vee
 \simeq V^\vee
\]

is nontrivial simple. Thus the image must vanish.

Next,

\[
q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0.
\tag{147.4}
\]

Otherwise quotienting \(T_0\) by the kernel of this linear restriction would give an extension

\[
0\longrightarrow \mathbf F_2t
\longrightarrow \overline M
\longrightarrow V
\longrightarrow0,
\qquad q(t)=1,
\]

with \(t\) in the polar radical. Any element in the kernel of the action on \(V\) could then act only by a shear \(x\mapsto x+f(\bar x)t\). Invariance of \(q\) forces \(f=0\), so the extension factors through the faithful tame image \(H_V\). But

\[
H^1(H_V,V^\vee)=0:
\]

for ramified \(V\), odd tame inertia has no invariants; for nontrivial unramified \(V\), the faithful image is cyclic of odd order. Hence the extension splits and yields a forbidden nonzero map \(M\to\mathbf F_2\), contradicting \((M^\vee)^C=0\).

Therefore \(q_\lambda\) descends to

\[
\bar q_\lambda:V\longrightarrow\mathbf F_2.
\]

It is nonzero, since otherwise \(K_\lambda\) would be elementary abelian and its Frattini central extension would split. Its polar radical is a submodule of the simple module \(V\); a zero polar form would make \(\bar q_\lambda\) an invariant linear functional. Hence \(\bar q_\lambda\) is nonsingular.

### 3. There is only one determinant orbit

Let \(q,q'\) be two nonzero invariant quadratic forms on \(V\), with polar forms \(b,b'\). Put

\[
E=\operatorname{End}_{H_V}(V),
\]

a finite field.

There is a unique \(a\in E^\times\) such that

\[
b'(v,w)=b(av,w).
\]

Symmetry gives \(a^*=a\) for the adjoint involution on \(E\). The norm \(u\mapsto u^*u\) is surjective onto the fixed field—ordinary finite-field norm when the involution is nontrivial, and the bijective square map when it is trivial. Choose \(u\) with \(u^*u=a\).

Then \(q(uv)\) and \(q'(v)\) have the same polar form. Their difference is an invariant linear functional, hence zero. Thus

\[
q'(v)=q(uv).
\tag{147.5}
\]

So all target-derived determinant forms lie in one
\(\operatorname{Aut}_{H_V}(V)\)-orbit. Candidate extraspecial evaluation and the local connecting construction are both natural under these isometries. No coefficient-field choice can introduce a second sign.

### 4. Candidate Wall sign

For a \(2\)-power-order \(U\in O(V,q)\), define

\[
q_U(x)=q(x)+b(x,U^{-1}x).
\]

Writing \(N=1+U\), orthogonality gives

\[
b(x,U^{-1}x)=b(x,Ux)=q(Nx).
\]

The Wall form on \(R_U=\operatorname{im}N\),

\[
\omega(Nx,u)=b(x,u),
\]

is well-defined and nondegenerate. Reducing
\((V,q_U)\perp(V,q)\) along the diagonal copy of \(\ker N\) gives the doubled Wall form

\[
\Phi_\omega(v,u)
 =\omega(v,v)+\omega(u,u)+\omega(v,u).
\]

Elementary elimination yields

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(\Phi_\omega)
 \equiv\dim R_U\pmod2,
\]

and therefore

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(q_U)
 =\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
 \pmod2.
\tag{147.6}
\]

For ramified \(V\), write the inertia degree and Hermitian multiplicity as

\[
f=2^ar,\qquad r\ \text{odd},\qquad s.
\]

The restriction of \(q\) is an orthogonal sum of \(s\) Hermitian norm–trace lines, so

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(q)\equiv s\pmod2.
\]

The operator \(U=S^{\omega _2}\) permutes the \(f\) inertia weights in \(r\) cycles of length \(2^a\), each with multiplicity \(s\). Its \(2^a\)-th power is a central \(2\)-element and hence acts trivially on the simple characteristic-two module. Thus

\[
\dim V^U=rs,
\qquad
\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
 =fs-rs
 \equiv s\pmod2.
\]

Equation (147.6) gives

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{h,q})=0
\]

for every ramified simple self-dual module.

For unramified \(V\), \(U=1\), so \(Q_{h,q}=q\). The unique invariant norm–trace form is minus type and has Arf invariant \(1\).

Therefore

\[
\sum_{c\in H^1_\rho(\Gamma_h,V)}
(-1)^{Q_{h,q}(c)}
=
\epsilon(V)\,2^{\dim V/2},
\qquad
\epsilon(V)=
\begin{cases}
-1,&V\ \text{unramified},\\
+1,&V\ \text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\tag{147.7}
\]

### 5. Local Kummer sign, including the omitted involution orbits

Use the faithful tame image \(H_V\) and its tame splitting field \(K/\mathbf Q_2\).

#### Unramified case

Here \(H_V\) is cyclic of odd order. If

\[
D=\operatorname{End}_{H_V}(V),\qquad D_0=D^{*},
\]

then \(H^1(\mathbf Q_2,V)\) is one-dimensional over \(D\). Naturality under the norm-one isometry group forces the local connecting form to be

\[
Q_{\mathrm{loc}}(x)
 =\operatorname{Tr}_{D_0/\mathbf F_2}(a\,xx^*),
 \qquad a\in D_0^\times.
\]

Its number of zeros is

\[
1+(2^m+1)(2^{m-1}-1)
 =2^{2m-1}-2^{m-1}.
\]

Thus the local unramified form is also minus type.

#### Ramified case

For the faithful image, a Sylow \(2\)-subgroup freely cycles the \(2\)-primary portions of the inertia-weight orbit. Hence \(V\) and \(V^\vee\) are projective \(\mathbf F_2[H_V]\)-modules. Therefore

\[
H^1(\mathbf Q_2,V)
 \simeq
 \operatorname{Hom}_{H_V}
 \bigl(V^\vee,K^\times/K^{\times2}\bigr),
\]

and \(\operatorname{Hom}_{H_V}(V^\vee,-)\) is exact on the unit filtration.

Let \(e=v_K(2)\) and

\[
X_+
 =\operatorname{Hom}_{H_V}(V^\vee,U_{e+1}).
\]

The perfect pairings between depths \(j\) and \(2e-j\) show

\[
\dim X_+=\frac12\dim H^1(\mathbf Q_2,V).
\]

It remains to check the quadratic refinement. Embedding \(V\) \(H_V\)-split into a free module reduces every quadratic monomial to one of three Shapiro orbit types:

1. square orbits;
2. product orbits with trivial stabilizer;
3. product orbits stabilized by an involution.

The first two vanish on \(U_{e+1}\) by

\[
U_i^\perp=U_{2e-i+1},
\qquad
\alpha^2=\alpha\cup(-1),
\qquad -1\in U_e.
\]

For the third type, the associated quadratic extension \(L/k\) is unramified. Its contribution is an index-two Evens norm. If

\[
\alpha=[a]\in U_{e+1}(L),\qquad a=1+2b,
\]

write

\[
u=1+\operatorname{Tr}(b),\qquad
n=N_{L/k}(a).
\]

The Stiefel–Whitney induction calculation gives

\[
N^{\mathrm{Ev}}_{L/k}(\alpha)
 =(u,-d)+(2du,n).
\tag{147.8}
\]

Now \(n\) has the same square class as \(2u-1\). Every unit of \(k\), and \(n\) itself, is a norm from the unramified extension. Hence (147.8) becomes

\[
(u,-1)+(2u,2u-1).
\]

Steinberg gives \((2u,2u-1)=(2u,-1)\), while
\((2,-1)=0\). The two summands are equal and cancel:

\[
N^{\mathrm{Ev}}_{L/k}(\alpha)=0.
\]

Thus all three orbit types vanish on \(X_+\). The deep subspace is half-dimensional and totally singular, so the local ramified determinant form is hyperbolic. This is the repaired Shapiro calculation from the attached sweep. 

Consequently,

\[
\sum_{c\in H^1(\mathbf Q_2,V)}
(-1)^{Q_{\mathrm{loc},q}(c)}
=
\epsilon(V)\,2^{\dim V/2}
\tag{147.9}
\]

with exactly the same \(\epsilon(V)\) as in (147.7).

### 6. Inner Fourier pushouts and shears introduce no new sign

For an inner radical Fourier label \(a\), the corresponding central cover class \(\alpha_a\) restricts trivially to \(V\): its preimage over \(V\) is a pushout of the elementary abelian extension

\[
0\longrightarrow T_0\longrightarrow M\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow0.
\]

Hence adding \(\alpha_a\) does not alter \(\bar q_\lambda\).

Because \(\bar q_\lambda\) is nonsingular, the transgression identity

\[
d_2(\bar q_\lambda)
 =b_{\bar q_\lambda}^{\flat}(\eta)
\]

forces the \(V\)-extension \(E\to C\) to split. The remaining filtration-one edge is removed by a shear that restricts to the identity on \(V\). Therefore the determinant form itself remains \(\bar q_\lambda\).

For every lower map \(\rho\), the exact pointwise identity is

\[
F_{\rho,\lambda}(c)
 +\langle a,h_\rho(c)\rangle
 =
Q_{\Gamma,\bar q_\lambda}
  \bigl(c+\rho^*u_{\lambda,a}\bigr)
 +\rho^*\Delta_{\lambda,a}.
\tag{147.10}
\]

Translation preserves the Gauss sum. The class
\(\Delta_{\lambda,a}\) is target-derived and is measured by a strictly smaller central target in the recurrence DAG. Thus neither radical accessibility, the second Fourier transform, nor marking-preserving shearing creates an additional determinant orbit or sign. This is precisely the functorial interface frozen in Turn 141. 

### Audited Proposition III

For every rank-one zero-edge node:

1. its outer scalar pushout descends to a nonzero nonsingular invariant quadratic form on the nontrivial simple head;
2. all such forms constitute one \(H_V\)-linear isometry orbit;
3. exact \(h_0\) extraspecial evaluation and the local Kummer connecting construction are natural on that orbit;
4. both sources have the common Gauss sum
   \[
   \epsilon(V)2^{\dim V/2},
   \quad
   \epsilon(V)=-1\ \text{unramified},\ 
   \epsilon(V)=+1\ \text{ramified};
   \]
5. all radical pushouts and shears preserve the simple-head form, translating only the lift variable and replacing an inflated constant by a strictly smaller target-derived class.

No new obstruction or correction to the frozen word appeared.

**ESTABLISHED**: Independently audited Proposition III from the exact \(h_0\) word through the simple-head descent, single-orbit theorem, Wall sign, faithful-image Kummer calculation, involution-stabilized Evens norms, and double-Fourier/shearing interface. Proposition III passes. Propositions I–III and the target recurrence shell now all survive their requested internal line-by-line audits.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the complete argument has not received an independent external manuscript review.

**NEXT**: Submit the frozen \(h_0\) block unchanged. On any verifier failure, construct the certificate for the first failing target and inspect the lowest rejected framed node; do not alter the word before that localization.
